behaviors.description: The primary translator produces a grammatically coherent translation that preserves source meaning, not just literal word substitution.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: the failure is not just local logic; it is whether model output quality is acceptable. REAL-INFRA: no OS/subprocess requirement is essential. MOCK-HIDE: a stubbed translator would always return curated text and hide true quality failures. LLM-DEP: yes, correctness depends on real neural translation output quality and variability. MULTI-STEP: single model decision, so not Workflow. REJECTION-GATE: rejected Workflow because behavior text covers one translation decision, not multiple agentic stages.
behaviors.test_description: Run the service against the real translation model on a curated multilingual semantic-equivalence set (idioms, tense, negation, named entities). Assert meaning-preservation and grammatical-acceptability thresholds (human rubric and/or semantic scorer) across repeated runs, failing if quality drops below threshold.

behaviors.description: When the translation API returns HTTP 503, the service transparently falls back to dictionary translation and does not return an error to the caller.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: bug appears at module handoff (API client -> fallback translator -> response layer), not a single pure function. REAL-INFRA: real network is not required; an in-process stub returning 503 reproduces the failure boundary. MOCK-HIDE: mocking does not inherently hide this if fallback wiring is real. LLM-DEP: no dependence on model output quality. MULTI-STEP: no multi-agentic chain. INTEGRATION-BRAKE checks: (a) no, (b) no, (c) no, (d) no. REJECTION-GATE: rejected System because behavior specifies a 503 response handling path, not real network timing/OS behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Wire real service modules with a fake primary API adapter that returns 503, plus a real fallback dictionary module. Call translation endpoint and assert: HTTP success to caller, fallback text returned, no surfaced upstream error, and fallback path telemetry/event emitted.

behaviors.description: The dictionary-based fallback returns the correct pre-stored translation for phrases present in SQLite.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: behavior spans phrase lookup logic plus DB access/mapping. REAL-INFRA: requires local DB interaction, but this is within Integration scope (local SQLite). MOCK-HIDE: replacing DB with map/dummy can hide schema/query/collation mistakes. LLM-DEP: none. MULTI-STEP: none. INTEGRATION-BRAKE checks: (a) no real external OS/network/subprocess needed beyond local DB use, (b) no, (c) no, (d) no. REJECTION-GATE: rejected Unit because behavior text explicitly requires SQLite-backed retrieval, not just pure lookup logic.
behaviors.test_description: Create an ephemeral SQLite DB with known phrase->translation rows, run fallback translator through normal repository layer, and assert exact returned translations for existing phrases (including normalization edge cases like casing/whitespace if supported).

behaviors.description: Quality tracking computes character-level edit distance between primary and fallback outputs and stores the metric.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: computation plus persistence is a module interaction boundary. REAL-INFRA: no real network/subprocess required; local persistence wiring is sufficient. MOCK-HIDE: mocking persistence can miss serialization/field-mapping bugs; pure unit test only catches algorithm math. LLM-DEP: none if fixed strings are provided. MULTI-STEP: none. INTEGRATION-BRAKE checks: (a) no, (b) no, (c) no, (d) no. REJECTION-GATE: rejected Agentic because behavior text is about deterministic edit-distance calculation/storage, not judging model output quality.
behaviors.test_description: Send fixed primary/fallback text pairs through the quality-tracking path with real repository wiring, assert exact edit-distance values and that the metric record is persisted with correct linkage/timestamp fields.

behaviors.description: When both translators are available, service returns primary translation and logs quality comparison in the background.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure is orchestration/state handoff (primary result selection + async quality logging). REAL-INFRA: can be reproduced with in-process async executor and local store; no mandatory real subprocess/network conditions stated. MOCK-HIDE: fully mocking background/logging can hide race or missed-enqueue defects, so keep real local wiring. LLM-DEP: behavior does not require evaluating model quality, only preference/routing and side-effect logging. MULTI-STEP: not a multi-agentic decision chain. INTEGRATION-BRAKE checks: (a) no required by behavior text, (b) no content-quality dependence, (c) no, (d) no. REJECTION-GATE: rejected Agentic because behavior text asserts routing/side effects, not correctness of model-generated content.
behaviors.test_description: With both translator adapters healthy (deterministic fixtures), call translate and assert immediate response equals primary output; then await/background-flush and assert quality comparison job executed and persisted without blocking caller latency budget.
